members.name: FontDrawGlyph
members.signature: public void FontDrawGlyph(RID fontRid, RID canvas, int size, Vector2 pos, int index, Color color, float oversampling)
members.summary: Draws single glyph into a canvas item at the position, using [param font_rid] at the size [param size]. If [param oversampling] is greater than zero, it is used as font oversampling factor, otherwise viewport oversampling settings are used. [b]Note:[/b] Glyph index is specific to the font, use glyphs indices returned by [method shaped_text_get_glyphs] or [method font_get_glyph_index]. [b]Note:[/b] If there are pending glyphs to render, calling this function might trigger the texture cache update.

[b]Note:[/b] Glyph index is specific to the font, use glyphs indices returned by [method shaped_text_get_glyphs] or [method font_get_glyph_index]. [b]Note:[/b] If there are pending glyphs to render, calling this function might trigger the texture cache update.

members.name: FontGetGlyphContours
members.signature: public Dictionary FontGetGlyphContours(RID font, int size, int index)
members.summary: Returns outline contours of the glyph as a [Dictionary] with the following contents: [code]points[/code] - [PackedVector3Array], containing outline points. [code]x[/code] and [code]y[/code] are point coordinates. [code]z[/code] is the type of the point, using the [enum ContourPointTag] values. [code]contours[/code] - [PackedInt32Array], containing indices the end points of each contour. [code]orientation[/code] - [bool], contour orientation. If [code]true[/code], clockwise contours must be filled. - Two successive [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_ON] points indicate a line segment. - One [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_OFF_CONIC] point between two [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_ON] points indicates a single conic (quadratic) Bézier arc. - Two [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_OFF_CUBIC] points between two [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_ON] points indicate a single cubic Bézier arc. - Two successive [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_OFF_CONIC] points indicate two successive conic (quadratic) Bézier arcs with a virtual [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_ON] point at their middle. - Each contour is closed. The last point of a contour uses the first point of a contour as its next point, and vice versa. The first point can be [constant CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_OFF_CONIC] point.

members.name: FontSetMultichannelSignedDistanceField
members.signature: public void FontSetMultichannelSignedDistanceField(RID fontRid, bool msdf)
members.summary: If set to [code]true[/code], glyphs of all sizes are rendered using single multichannel signed distance field generated from the dynamic font vector data. MSDF rendering allows displaying the font at any scaling factor without blurriness, and without incurring a CPU cost when the font size changes (since the font no longer needs to be rasterized on the CPU). As a downside, font hinting is not available with MSDF. The lack of font hinting may result in less crisp and less readable fonts at small sizes. [b]Note:[/b] MSDF font rendering does not render glyphs with overlapping shapes correctly. Overlapping shapes are not valid per the OpenType standard, but are still commonly found in many font files, especially those converted by Google Fonts. To avoid issues with overlapping glyphs, consider downloading the font file directly from the type foundry instead of relying on Google Fonts.

members.signature: public bool IsValidIdentifier(String @string)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param string] is a valid identifier. If the text server supports the [constant FEATURE_UNICODE_IDENTIFIERS] feature, a valid identifier must: - Conform to normalization form C. - Begin with a Unicode character of class XID_Start or [code]"_"[/code]. - May contain Unicode characters of class XID_Continue in the other positions. - Use UAX #31 recommended scripts only (mixed scripts are allowed). If the [constant FEATURE_UNICODE_IDENTIFIERS] feature is not supported, a valid identifier must: - Begin with a Unicode character of class XID_Start or [code]"_"[/code]. - May contain Unicode characters of class XID_Continue in the other positions.

members.name: ShapedTextDraw
members.signature: public void ShapedTextDraw(RID shaped, RID canvas, Vector2 pos, float clipL, float clipR, Color color, float oversampling)
members.summary: Draw shaped text into a canvas item at a given position, with [param color]. [param pos] specifies the leftmost point of the baseline (for horizontal layout) or topmost point of the baseline (for vertical layout). If [param oversampling] is greater than zero, it is used as font oversampling factor, otherwise viewport oversampling settings are used. [param clip_l] and [param clip_r] are offsets relative to [param pos], going to the right in horizontal layout and downward in vertical layout. If [param clip_l] is not negative, glyphs starting before the offset are clipped. If [param clip_r] is not negative, glyphs ending after the offset are clipped.

members.summary: Draw the outline of the shaped text into a canvas item at a given position, with [param color]. [param pos] specifies the leftmost point of the baseline (for horizontal layout) or topmost point of the baseline (for vertical layout). If [param oversampling] is greater than zero, it is used as font oversampling factor, otherwise viewport oversampling settings are used. [param clip_l] and [param clip_r] are offsets relative to [param pos], going to the right in horizontal layout and downward in vertical layout. If [param clip_l] is not negative, glyphs starting before the offset are clipped. If [param clip_r] is not negative, glyphs ending after the offset are clipped.

members.name: StringGetWordBreaks
members.signature: public PackedInt32Array StringGetWordBreaks(String @string, String language, int charsPerLine)
members.summary: Returns an array of the word break boundaries. Elements in the returned array are the offsets of the start and end of words. Therefore the length of the array is always even. When [param chars_per_line] is greater than zero, line break boundaries are returned instead. [codeblock] var ts = TextServerManager.get_primary_interface() # Corresponds to the substrings "The", "Godot", "Engine", and "4". print(ts.string_get_word_breaks("The Godot Engine, 4")) # Prints [0, 3, 4, 9, 10, 16, 18, 19] # Corresponds to the substrings "The", "Godot", "Engin", and "e, 4". print(ts.string_get_word_breaks("The Godot Engine, 4", "en", 5)) # Prints [0, 3, 4, 9, 10, 15, 15, 19] # Corresponds to the substrings "The Godot" and "Engine, 4". print(ts.string_get_word_breaks("The Godot Engine, 4", "en", 10)) # Prints [0, 9, 10, 19] [/codeblock]

values.name: AUTOWRAP_ARBITRARY
values.summary: Wraps the text inside the node's bounding rectangle by allowing to break lines at arbitrary positions, which is useful when very limited space is available.

values.name: AUTOWRAP_WORD
values.summary: Wraps the text inside the node's bounding rectangle by soft-breaking between words.

values.name: AUTOWRAP_WORD_SMART
values.summary: Behaves similarly to [constant AUTOWRAP_WORD], but force-breaks a word if that single word does not fit in one line.

values.summary: Contour point isn't on the curve, but serves as a control point for a conic (quadratic) Bézier arc.

values.name: DIRECTION_AUTO

values.name: FEATURE_BIDI_LAYOUT

values.summary: TextServer supports multichannel signed distance field dynamic font rendering.

values.summary: TextServer supports loading system fonts.

values.summary: TextServer supports variable fonts.

values.name: FEATURE_SIMPLE_LAYOUT

values.name: FEATURE_VERTICAL_LAYOUT

values.name: FIXED_SIZE_SCALE_DISABLE
values.summary: Bitmap font is not scaled.

values.name: FIXED_SIZE_SCALE_ENABLED
values.summary: Bitmap font is scaled to an arbitrary (fractional) size. This is the recommended option for non-pixel art fonts.

values.name: FIXED_SIZE_SCALE_INTEGER_ONLY
values.summary: Bitmap font is scaled to the closest integer multiple of the font's fixed size. This is the recommended option for pixel art fonts.

values.name: FONT_ANTIALIASING_GRAY
values.summary: Font glyphs are rasterized as 8-bit grayscale anti-aliased bitmaps.

values.name: FONT_ANTIALIASING_LCD
values.summary: Font glyphs are rasterized for LCD screens. LCD subpixel layout is determined by the value of the [member ProjectSettings.gui/theme/lcd_subpixel_layout] setting. LCD subpixel anti-aliasing mode is suitable only for rendering horizontal, unscaled text in 2D.

values.name: FONT_ANTIALIASING_NONE

values.name: FONT_LCD_SUBPIXEL_LAYOUT_HBGR

values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum FontLCDSubpixelLayout] enum.

values.name: FONT_LCD_SUBPIXEL_LAYOUT_NONE
values.summary: Unknown or unsupported subpixel layout, LCD subpixel antialiasing is disabled.

values.name: FONT_LCD_SUBPIXEL_LAYOUT_VBGR
values.summary: Vertical BGR subpixel layout.

values.name: FONT_LCD_SUBPIXEL_LAYOUT_VRGB
values.summary: Vertical RGB subpixel layout.

values.name: FONT_BOLD
values.summary: Font is bold.

values.name: FONT_FIXED_WIDTH
values.summary: Font has fixed-width characters (also known as monospace).

values.name: FONT_ITALIC
values.summary: Font is italic or oblique.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_BREAK_HARD
values.summary: Grapheme is mandatory break point (e.g. [code]"\n"[/code]).

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_BREAK_SOFT
values.summary: Grapheme is optional break point (e.g. space).

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_CONNECTED
values.summary: Grapheme is connected to the previous grapheme. Breaking line before this grapheme is not safe.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_ELONGATION
values.summary: Grapheme is kashida.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_EMBEDDED_OBJECT
values.summary: Grapheme is an object replacement character for the embedded object.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_PUNCTUATION
values.summary: Grapheme is punctuation character.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_RTL
values.summary: Grapheme is part of right-to-left or bottom-to-top run.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_SAFE_TO_INSERT_TATWEEL
values.summary: It is safe to insert a U+0640 before this grapheme for elongation.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_SOFT_HYPHEN
values.summary: Grapheme is a soft hyphen.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_SPACE
values.summary: Grapheme is whitespace.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_TAB
values.summary: Grapheme is the tabulation character.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_UNDERSCORE
values.summary: Grapheme is underscore character.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_VALID
values.summary: Grapheme is supported by the font, and can be drawn.

values.name: GRAPHEME_IS_VIRTUAL
values.summary: Grapheme is not part of source text, it was added by justification process.

values.name: HINTING_LIGHT
values.summary: Use the light font hinting mode.

values.name: HINTING_NONE
values.summary: Disables font hinting (smoother but less crisp).

values.name: HINTING_NORMAL
values.summary: Use the default font hinting mode (crisper but less smooth). [b]Note:[/b] This hinting mode changes both horizontal and vertical glyph metrics. If applied to monospace font, some glyphs might have different width.

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_AFTER_LAST_TAB
values.summary: Only apply justification to the part of the text after the last tab.

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_CONSTRAIN_ELLIPSIS
values.summary: Apply justification to the trimmed line with ellipsis.

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_DO_NOT_SKIP_SINGLE_LINE
values.summary: Always apply justification to the paragraphs with a single line ([constant JUSTIFICATION_SKIP_LAST_LINE] and [constant JUSTIFICATION_SKIP_LAST_LINE_WITH_VISIBLE_CHARS] are ignored).

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_KASHIDA
values.summary: Justify text by adding and removing kashidas.

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_NONE
values.summary: Do not justify text.

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_SKIP_LAST_LINE
values.summary: Do not apply justification to the last line of the paragraph.

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_SKIP_LAST_LINE_WITH_VISIBLE_CHARS
values.summary: Do not apply justification to the last line of the paragraph with visible characters (takes precedence over [constant JUSTIFICATION_SKIP_LAST_LINE]).

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_TRIM_EDGE_SPACES
values.summary: Remove trailing and leading spaces from the justified text.

values.name: JUSTIFICATION_WORD_BOUND
values.summary: Justify text by changing width of the spaces between the words.

values.name: BREAK_ADAPTIVE
values.summary: Should be used only in conjunction with [constant BREAK_WORD_BOUND], break the line between any unconnected graphemes, if it's impossible to break it between the words.

values.name: BREAK_GRAPHEME_BOUND
values.summary: Break the line between any unconnected graphemes.

values.name: BREAK_MANDATORY
values.summary: Break the line at the line mandatory break characters (e.g. [code]"\n"[/code]).

values.name: BREAK_NONE
values.summary: Do not break the line.

values.name: BREAK_TRIM_EDGE_SPACES
values.summary: Remove edge spaces from the broken line segments.

values.name: BREAK_TRIM_END_EDGE_SPACES
values.summary: Remove spaces and line break characters from the end of broken line segments. E.g, after line breaking, the first segment of the following text [code]test \n next[/code], is [code]test[/code] if the flag is set, and [code]test \n[/code] if it is not.

values.name: BREAK_TRIM_INDENT
values.summary: Subtract first line indentation width from all lines after the first one.

values.name: BREAK_TRIM_START_EDGE_SPACES
values.summary: Remove spaces and line break characters from the start of broken line segments. E.g, after line breaking, the second segment of the following text [code]test \n next[/code], is [code]next[/code] if the flag is set, and [code] next[/code] if it is not.

values.name: BREAK_WORD_BOUND
values.summary: Break the line between the words.

values.name: ORIENTATION_HORIZONTAL
values.summary: Text is written horizontally.

values.name: ORIENTATION_VERTICAL
values.summary: Left to right text is written vertically from top to bottom. Right to left text is written vertically from bottom to top.

values.name: OVERRUN_NO_TRIMMING
values.summary: No text trimming is performed.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM_CHAR
values.summary: Trims the text per character.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM_ELLIPSIS
values.summary: Trims the text per character and adds an ellipsis to indicate that parts are hidden if trimmed text is 6 characters or longer.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM_ELLIPSIS_FORCE
values.summary: Trims the text per character and adds an ellipsis to indicate that parts are hidden regardless of trimmed text length.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM_WORD
values.summary: Trims the text per word.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM_WORD_ELLIPSIS
values.summary: Trims the text per word and adds an ellipsis to indicate that parts are hidden if trimmed text is 6 characters or longer.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM_WORD_ELLIPSIS_FORCE
values.summary: Trims the text per word and adds an ellipsis to indicate that parts are hidden regardless of trimmed text length.

values.name: SPACING_BOTTOM
values.summary: Spacing at the bottom of the line.

values.name: SPACING_GLYPH
values.summary: Spacing for each glyph.

values.name: SPACING_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SpacingType] enum.

values.name: SPACING_SPACE
values.summary: Spacing for the space character.

values.name: SPACING_TOP
values.summary: Spacing at the top of the line.

values.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_CUSTOM
values.summary: User defined structured text BiDi override function.

values.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_DEFAULT
values.summary: Use default Unicode BiDi algorithm.

values.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_EMAIL
values.summary: BiDi override for email.

values.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_FILE
values.summary: BiDi override for file path.

values.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_GDSCRIPT
values.summary: BiDi override for GDScript.

values.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_LIST
values.summary: BiDi override for lists. Structured text options: list separator [String].

values.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_URI
values.summary: BiDi override for URI.

values.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_AUTO
values.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded based on font size. - To one quarter of the pixel size if font size is smaller or equal to [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_QUARTER_MAX_SIZE]. - To one half of the pixel size if font size is smaller or equal to [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_HALF_MAX_SIZE]. - To the whole pixel size for larger fonts.

values.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_DISABLED
values.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded to the whole pixel size, each glyph is rasterized once.

values.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_HALF
values.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded to one half of the pixel size, each glyph is rasterized up to two times.

values.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_HALF_MAX_SIZE
values.summary: Maximum font size which will use "one half of the pixel" subpixel positioning in [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_AUTO] mode.

values.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_QUARTER
values.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded to one quarter of the pixel size, each glyph is rasterized up to four times.

values.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_QUARTER_MAX_SIZE
values.summary: Maximum font size which will use "one quarter of the pixel" subpixel positioning in [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_AUTO] mode.

values.name: OVERRUN_ADD_ELLIPSIS
values.summary: Determines whether an ellipsis should be added at the end of the text.

values.name: OVERRUN_ENFORCE_ELLIPSIS
values.summary: Determines whether the ellipsis at the end of the text is enforced and may not be hidden.

values.name: OVERRUN_JUSTIFICATION_AWARE
values.summary: Accounts for the text being justified before attempting to trim it (see [enum JustificationFlag]).

values.name: OVERRUN_NO_TRIM
values.summary: No trimming is performed.

values.name: OVERRUN_SHORT_STRING_ELLIPSIS
values.summary: Determines whether the ellipsis should be added regardless of the string length, otherwise it is added only if the string is 6 characters or longer.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM
values.summary: Trims the text when it exceeds the given width.

values.name: OVERRUN_TRIM_WORD_ONLY
values.summary: Trims the text per word instead of per grapheme.

values.name: VC_CHARS_AFTER_SHAPING
values.summary: Displays glyphs that are mapped to the first [member Label.visible_characters] or [member RichTextLabel.visible_characters] characters from the beginning of the text.

values.name: VC_CHARS_BEFORE_SHAPING
values.summary: Trims text before the shaping. e.g, increasing [member Label.visible_characters] or [member RichTextLabel.visible_characters] value is visually identical to typing the text. [b]Note:[/b] In this mode, trimmed text is not processed at all. It is not accounted for in line breaking and size calculations.

values.name: VC_GLYPHS_AUTO
values.summary: Displays [member Label.visible_ratio] or [member RichTextLabel.visible_ratio] glyphs, starting from the left or from the right, depending on [member Control.layout_direction] value.

values.name: VC_GLYPHS_LTR
values.summary: Displays [member Label.visible_ratio] or [member RichTextLabel.visible_ratio] glyphs, starting from the left.

values.name: VC_GLYPHS_RTL
values.summary: Displays [member Label.visible_ratio] or [member RichTextLabel.visible_ratio] glyphs, starting from the right.

constants.name: GRAPHEME_IS_ELONGATION
constants.summary: Grapheme is kashida.

constants.name: GRAPHEME_IS_PUNCTUATION
constants.summary: Grapheme is punctuation character.

constants.name: GRAPHEME_IS_UNDERSCORE
constants.summary: Grapheme is underscore character.

constants.name: GRAPHEME_IS_CONNECTED
constants.summary: Grapheme is connected to the previous grapheme. Breaking line before this grapheme is not safe.

constants.name: GRAPHEME_IS_SAFE_TO_INSERT_TATWEEL
constants.summary: It is safe to insert a U+0640 before this grapheme for elongation.

constants.name: GRAPHEME_IS_EMBEDDED_OBJECT
constants.summary: Grapheme is an object replacement character for the embedded object.

constants.name: GRAPHEME_IS_SOFT_HYPHEN
constants.summary: Grapheme is a soft hyphen.

constants.name: HINTING_NONE
constants.summary: Disables font hinting (smoother but less crisp).

constants.name: HINTING_LIGHT
constants.summary: Use the light font hinting mode.

constants.name: HINTING_NORMAL
constants.summary: Use the default font hinting mode (crisper but less smooth). [b]Note:[/b] This hinting mode changes both horizontal and vertical glyph metrics. If applied to monospace font, some glyphs might have different width.

constants.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_DISABLED
constants.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded to the whole pixel size, each glyph is rasterized once.

constants.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_AUTO
constants.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded based on font size. - To one quarter of the pixel size if font size is smaller or equal to [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_QUARTER_MAX_SIZE]. - To one half of the pixel size if font size is smaller or equal to [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_HALF_MAX_SIZE]. - To the whole pixel size for larger fonts.

constants.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_HALF
constants.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded to one half of the pixel size, each glyph is rasterized up to two times.

constants.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_QUARTER
constants.summary: Glyph horizontal position is rounded to one quarter of the pixel size, each glyph is rasterized up to four times.

constants.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_HALF_MAX_SIZE
constants.summary: Maximum font size which will use "one half of the pixel" subpixel positioning in [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_AUTO] mode.

constants.name: SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_ONE_QUARTER_MAX_SIZE
constants.summary: Maximum font size which will use "one quarter of the pixel" subpixel positioning in [constant SUBPIXEL_POSITIONING_AUTO] mode.

constants.name: FEATURE_SIMPLE_LAYOUT
constants.summary: TextServer supports simple text layouts.

constants.name: FEATURE_BIDI_LAYOUT
constants.summary: TextServer supports bidirectional text layouts.

constants.name: FEATURE_VERTICAL_LAYOUT
constants.summary: TextServer supports vertical layouts.

constants.name: FEATURE_SHAPING
constants.summary: TextServer supports complex text shaping.

constants.name: FEATURE_KASHIDA_JUSTIFICATION
constants.summary: TextServer supports justification using kashidas.

constants.name: FEATURE_BREAK_ITERATORS
constants.summary: TextServer supports complex line/word breaking rules (e.g. dictionary based).

constants.name: FEATURE_FONT_BITMAP
constants.summary: TextServer supports loading bitmap fonts.

constants.name: FEATURE_FONT_DYNAMIC
constants.summary: TextServer supports loading dynamic (TrueType, OpeType, etc.) fonts.

constants.name: FEATURE_FONT_MSDF
constants.summary: TextServer supports multichannel signed distance field dynamic font rendering.

constants.name: FEATURE_FONT_SYSTEM
constants.summary: TextServer supports loading system fonts.

constants.name: FEATURE_FONT_VARIABLE
constants.summary: TextServer supports variable fonts.

constants.name: FEATURE_CONTEXT_SENSITIVE_CASE_CONVERSION
constants.summary: TextServer supports locale dependent and context sensitive case conversion.

constants.name: FEATURE_USE_SUPPORT_DATA
constants.summary: TextServer require external data file for some features, see [method load_support_data].

constants.name: FEATURE_UNICODE_IDENTIFIERS
constants.summary: TextServer supports UAX #31 identifier validation, see [method is_valid_identifier].

constants.name: FEATURE_UNICODE_SECURITY
constants.summary: TextServer supports [url=https://unicode.org/reports/tr36/]Unicode Technical Report #36[/url] and [url=https://unicode.org/reports/tr39/]Unicode Technical Standard #39[/url] based spoof detection features.

constants.name: CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_ON
constants.summary: Contour point is on the curve.

constants.name: CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_OFF_CONIC
constants.summary: Contour point isn't on the curve, but serves as a control point for a conic (quadratic) Bézier arc.

constants.name: CONTOUR_CURVE_TAG_OFF_CUBIC
constants.summary: Contour point isn't on the curve, but serves as a control point for a cubic Bézier arc.

constants.name: SPACING_GLYPH
constants.summary: Spacing for each glyph.

constants.name: SPACING_SPACE
constants.summary: Spacing for the space character.

constants.name: SPACING_TOP
constants.summary: Spacing at the top of the line.

constants.name: SPACING_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Spacing at the bottom of the line.

constants.name: SPACING_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SpacingType] enum.

constants.name: FONT_BOLD
constants.summary: Font is bold.

constants.name: FONT_ITALIC
constants.summary: Font is italic or oblique.

constants.name: FONT_FIXED_WIDTH
constants.summary: Font has fixed-width characters (also known as monospace).

constants.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_DEFAULT
constants.summary: Use default Unicode BiDi algorithm.

constants.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_URI
constants.summary: BiDi override for URI.

constants.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_FILE
constants.summary: BiDi override for file path.

constants.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_EMAIL
constants.summary: BiDi override for email.

constants.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_LIST
constants.summary: BiDi override for lists. Structured text options: list separator [String].

constants.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_GDSCRIPT
constants.summary: BiDi override for GDScript.

constants.name: STRUCTURED_TEXT_CUSTOM
constants.summary: User defined structured text BiDi override function.

constants.name: FIXED_SIZE_SCALE_DISABLE
constants.summary: Bitmap font is not scaled.

constants.name: FIXED_SIZE_SCALE_INTEGER_ONLY
constants.summary: Bitmap font is scaled to the closest integer multiple of the font's fixed size. This is the recommended option for pixel art fonts.

constants.name: FIXED_SIZE_SCALE_ENABLED
constants.summary: Bitmap font is scaled to an arbitrary (fractional) size. This is the recommended option for non-pixel art fonts.
